title: jasminum longipetalum
intro: Jasminum longipetalum, also known as the Long-petaled Jasmine, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The Long-petaled Jasmine has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and essential oils, and is also dried and used in traditional remedies to promote relaxation and well-being.
